fix(fsi): add --multiemit- to prevent cross-submission TypeLoadException

FSI's default --multiemit+ creates separate dynamic assemblies per
EvalInteraction call. MonoMod's JIT hook (loaded by Expecto via Harmony)
throws TypeLoadException when types from one assembly are referenced in
another. This made records, DUs, and with-expressions fail across REPL
submissions.

Fix: add --multiemit- to FSI args in both code paths (solutionToFsiArgs
and DirectoryConfig eval). All submissions now share one dynamic assembly,
allowing types to be referenced across submissions.

Trade-off: types cannot be REDEFINED in subsequent submissions (duplicate
type name error), but for a REPL tool with finite session lifetimes and
hard-reset capability, this is the right trade-off.

13 TDD tests: 3 unit (args correctness) + 5 integration (record,
with-expression, DU, anon record, function) + 5 property (FsCheck:
N-field records, N-case DUs, int bindings, N-field anon records,
with-expression preservation).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Security hardening Phase 1 — critical auth & session fixes (#27, #28, #29, #33, #38) (#51)

* fix: security hardening — session ID entropy, XFF spoofing, rate limiter cap, timing attack, session resume auth (#27, #28, #29, #33, #38)

Phase 1 security fixes with TDD:

- #33: Replace mt19937 PRNG with direct std::random_device reads (128-bit
  OS CSPRNG entropy). Extract to server/session_id.hpp.
- #28: Extract IP extraction to server/ip_extraction.hpp with trust_proxy
  flag. Only reads X-Forwarded-For when trust_proxy=true, takes rightmost
  IP. Default: socket address only.
- #29: Add max_tracked_ips cap and evict_to_cap() to AuthRateLimiter.
  Evicts expired entries first, then lowest-failure entries. Add size()
  accessor.
- #27: Add constant-time string comparison (server/constant_time.hpp) for
  API key validation. Prevents timing side-channel attacks.
- #38: Validate session exists in SessionManager before allowing resume.
  Reject resume requests for non-existent sessions.

23 new tests (232 → 255), all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR #51 review feedback — 18 comments resolved

Review fixes:
- constant_time: XOR length mismatch into result instead of early return
  (prevents key length leak via timing). Add volatile rationale comment,
  <cstddef> include, use std::size_t.
- session_id: Use 4 rd() calls extracting 4 bytes each (4× more efficient).
  Replace ostringstream with zero-allocation hex table.
- ip_extraction: Add trusted_hops parameter for multi-proxy deployments.
  Trim HTAB per RFC 7239 OWS. Document single-proxy assumption.
- auth_rate_limiter: Compute return value BEFORE eviction to avoid dangling
  reference UB. Switch eviction to oldest window_start (LRU-like) to prevent
  bypass via distributed low-failure IPs. Fix comment/code mismatch.
- websocket_server: Fix inverted session resume check — reject if session ID
  already exists (conflict), not if it doesn't. Wire trust_proxy,
  trusted_proxy_hops, max_tracked_ips through config.hpp and main.cpp with
  WSS_TRUST_PROXY, WSS_TRUSTED_PROXY_HOPS, WSS_MAX_TRACKED_IPS env vars.
- test_session_id: Add missing <cctype> include.
- test_ip_extraction: Add multi-proxy and HTAB whitespace tests.
- test_rate_limiter_capacity: Adapt to oldest-eviction strategy.

258 tests passing (232 baseline + 26 new).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: dd on last empty line now deletes the trailing newline

line_range() returned an empty range when the cursor was on the
final empty line (start == end == len_chars). Vim deletes the
preceding newline in this case, removing the empty line and
moving the cursor up.

Fix: when the computed range is empty and we're not on line 0,
extend the range backward to include the previous line's trailing
newline.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix custom attribute encoding for generative type providers (#463)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

Two related fixes to custom attribute encoding in the generative type
provider assembly compiler (`AssemblyCompiler` / `defineCustomAttrs`).

## Fix 1: Implement array support in `encodeCustomAttrElemTypeForObject`

**Before:** Passing an `obj[]` (or typed array like `string[]`) as an
argument to an `Object`-typed custom attribute constructor parameter
would throw:
```
failwith "TODO: can't yet emit arrays in attrs"
```

**After:** Infers the element type from the runtime array type and
encodes `SZARRAY + element type byte` per ECMA-335 §II.23.3. Supported
element types: `string`, `bool`, `char`, `sbyte`/`byte`,
`int16`/`uint16`, `int32`/`uint32`, `int64`/`uint64`, `single`,
`double`, and `obj` (encoded as `OBJECT`).

## Fix 2: Apply `transValue` to all custom attribute argument values

**Before:** `transValue` (which translates `System.Type` → `ILType`) was
defined inside `defineCustomAttrs` but never used — the function was
dead code. Constructor arguments and named properties/fields were passed
through untranslated.

**After:** `transValue` is moved before its first use and applied to:
- All constructor fixed arguments (`constructorArgs`)
- Named property values (`namedProps`)
- Named field values (`namedFields`)

This ensures that if a custom attribute argument contains a
`System.Type` value (e.g., `typeof(SomeProvidedType)`), it is translated
to the target `ILType` and the correct type name is encoded in the
binary attribute blob.

## Root cause

Both issues affect **generative type providers** only. Erased providers
reflect attributes directly without going through ECMA-335 binary
encoding.

## Test Status

All existing tests pass: **104 passed, 0 failed** on net8.0.

```
Passed!  - Failed: 0, Passed: 104, Skipped: 0, Total: 104
````

Note: the new functionality (array args, Type args in attrs) is
exercised by the fixes themselves but no dedicated integration test is
added — adding one would require a new generative provider test fixture
with attributes using these specific parameter patterns.

commit.message:
fix: use n8n_session_id to look up chat history in session handoff

Chat history is indexed by n8n_session_id (the stable user-facing session
identifier) but export_transcript() and write_handoff_summary() were
calling _get_session_messages() with the internal copilot/claude session_id
(a UUID that changes on every runtime switch). This caused both methods to
return empty lists, silently skip writing any handoff files, and the
[Handoff] log line was misleading - it fired but nothing was written.

Fix: pass n8n_session_id to _get_session_messages() in both methods.

Verified: handoff.md and transcript.md now created correctly on runtime
switch. First message to new runtime receives injected context. Secret word
PINEAPPLE correctly recalled by gemini after switching from copilot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
cfl: CFL-075 EvaluateProfile iteration cap (timeout fix)

Cap nGran^ndim iterations in CIccEvalCompare::EvaluateProfile() to 100K.
A 6-channel profile with nGran=33 produces 33^6 = 1.29B iterations,
each calling Apply() twice — causing >1675s timeout.

Fix: dynamically reduce nGran so total iterations stay under 100K.
For ndim=6, nGran drops from 33 to ~6 (6^6=46656 iterations, ~3s).

This is a legitimate upstream algorithmic issue — iccRoundTrip also
hangs on this profile (verified via iccDEV/Build/Tools/).

CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix buffer stride calculation test and mark Phase 2.2 complete

Phase 2.2 (Buffer Allocator) was already implemented but had a failing test.
The test incorrectly expected X-tiled stride alignment to round up 7680 to
8192, when 7680 is already aligned to 512 bytes (7680 / 512 = 15 exactly).

Changes:
- Fixed allocator test to correctly expect 7680 for already-aligned values
- Added additional test cases for unaligned widths to verify alignment works
- All 24 Rust tests now pass
- All 20 Go test packages pass

Implementation complete:
✅ BufferAllocator: Allocates GPU buffers with X/Y-tiled formats (Intel i915/Xe)
✅ DMA-BUF export: export_dmabuf() provides fd for Wayland/X11 sharing
✅ SlabAllocator: Sub-allocation with first-fit strategy and coalescing

commit.message:
docs: add missing README coverage for after-pr workflow (#47)

PR #45 added `after-pr` workflow support and the `{pr.number}` /
`{pr.url}` template variables, but the README did not describe them.
This updates the docs so the shipped behavior and configuration
reference are aligned.

- **Workflow docs**
- Update the top-level product summary and session lifecycle to mention
post-PR automation.
- Clarify that `cruise run` can execute `after-pr` steps after
successful PR creation.

- **Config reference**
  - Extend the basic YAML structure to include `after-pr`.
- Add a dedicated `after-pr` section describing when these steps run and
that they use the same schema as normal workflow steps.

- **Template variables**
  - Document `{pr.number}` and `{pr.url}` in the variable reference.
  - Make it explicit that these values are available after PR creation.

- **Examples**
  - Add a focused `after-pr` example:
    ```yaml
    after-pr:
      notify:
        command: "echo 'PR #{pr.number} created: {pr.url}'"

      label:
        command: "gh pr edit {pr.number} --add-label enhancement"
    ```
- Update the full workflow example to show `after-pr` usage in context.
